case.title: The Dead Aim
case.subtitle: A multi-floor Topgolf murder where route logic matters as much as motive.

finalQuestions.label: Why did the killer do it?
options: To stop Nolan from exposing her theft and the larger nightlife pattern
To bury a VIP comp scandal

finalQuestions.label: How was the murder committed?
options: Nolan was shoved during an ordinary corridor fight
Nolan was drugged through his drink, lured downstairs, and staged into a fatal impact
A faulty cart mechanism caused the death
Nolan collapsed naturally and the scene only looked suspicious

finalQuestions.label: Which evidence best proves the death was staged and not random?
options: The medical finding that Nolan was incapacitated before impact

hints.1: The accident story fails where the drink and the impact stop matching each other.
hints.2: In a three-floor venue, motive matters less than whether the route makes sense.
hints.3: The best suspect can touch both the drink and the quiet corridor without seeming misplaced.
hints.4: Ask what Nolan's body was already failing to do before the metal edge ever mattered.
hints.5: The real motive must explain both simple theft and why Nolan's notes had become more dangerous than ordinary accounting.

suspects.name: Vanessa Cole
suspects.relationship: Worked closely with Nolan on event logistics
suspects.status_late: Too mobile, too prepared, motive widening fast

suspects.name: Marcus Reed
suspects.relationship: Nolan had written him up for mistakes and lateness
suspects.status_early: Looks terrible because the body is near his zone

suspects.name: Elise Benton
suspects.relationship: Nolan threatened to report her for off-book comps
suspects.status_early: Highly mobile, highly polished, hard to trust

suspects.name: Trent Holloway
suspects.relationship: Repeated clashes with Nolan over staffing and delays
suspects.status_early: Physically plausible and angry enough to fit the scene
suspects.status_late: Bad attitude, but route and timing narrow him

suspects.name: Jordan Pike
suspects.relationship: Nolan threatened a formal complaint after prior guest incidents
suspects.status_early: Has access to blind spots and building movement
suspects.status_late: Lie matters, murder does not fit

evidence_cart_edge.title: Dented Metal Cart Edge
evidence_cart_edge.desc: Metal cart edge near the body shows impact marks inconsistent with a clean running fall.
evidence_cart_edge.short: Impact geometry looks wrong for a spontaneous accident.

evidence_espresso_can.title: Espresso Cocktail Can
evidence_espresso_can.desc: Half-finished canned espresso cocktail recovered from a nearby service trash bin with residue inside the lip seam.
evidence_espresso_can.short: The drink can may be the delivery method.

evidence_phone_ping.title: Phone Message Ping
evidence_phone_ping.desc: Nolan received a message reading: 'Need you downstairs now. Service routing issue.'

evidence_bartender_handoff.title: Bartender Handoff Statement
evidence_bartender_handoff.desc: Ava saw Nolan take a canned espresso cocktail while briefly interacting with Vanessa.
evidence_bartender_handoff.short: Vanessa was near Nolan during the drink window.

evidence_corridor_support_sighting.title: Partial Corridor Support Sighting
evidence_corridor_support_sighting.desc: Priya saw a staff-clad figure guiding or supporting a stumbling person in the Level 1 back corridor.
evidence_corridor_support_sighting.short: Nolan may have been impaired before the impact.

evidence_medical_incapacitation_flag.title: Medical Incapacitation Flag
evidence_medical_incapacitation_flag.desc: Dr. Forsythe concludes Nolan likely lost coordination before the fatal strike.
evidence_medical_incapacitation_flag.short: Nolan was compromised before he hit the metal edge.

evidence_inventory_variance_sheet.title: Inventory Variance Sheet
evidence_inventory_variance_sheet.desc: Nolan marked repeated beverage discrepancies tied to event-night inventory under Vanessa's oversight.
evidence_inventory_variance_sheet.short: Nolan was tracking Vanessa's inventory problems.

evidence_symbol_ledger_photo.title: Symbol Marked Ledger Photo
evidence_symbol_ledger_photo.desc: Nolan photographed a ledger page marked with the same black symbol tied to prior nightlife suspicion.
evidence_symbol_ledger_photo.short: The same symbol from the previous case appears again.

evidence_elevator_delay_log.title: Elevator Delay Log
evidence_elevator_delay_log.desc: Elevator delay records make Vanessa's claimed route less efficient than a staff stairwell path.
evidence_elevator_delay_log.short: Vanessa's route story may not be the route she used.

evidence_maintenance_call_timestamp.title: Maintenance Call Timestamp
evidence_maintenance_call_timestamp.desc: Marcus received a real work ping close to the key window.
evidence_maintenance_call_timestamp.short: Marcus was not as free as he first appeared.

evidence_vip_wristband_record.title: VIP Wristband Record
evidence_vip_wristband_record.desc: Guest scans partially support Elise's movement with VIPs between Level 3 and Level 2.
evidence_vip_wristband_record.short: Elise's most suspicious movement has some support.

evidence_kitchen_order_snapshot.title: Kitchen Order Rail Snapshot
evidence_kitchen_order_snapshot.desc: Kitchen rail timing supports Trent being tied to a service backlog during part of the key window.
evidence_kitchen_order_snapshot.short: Trent was not fully free during the whole window.

evidence_blind_spot_map.title: Security Camera Blind Spot Map
evidence_blind_spot_map.desc: Map of coverage gaps shows a Level 1 corridor where staff movement could go unseen.
evidence_blind_spot_map.short: The building has a corridor built for quiet movement.

evidence_notebook.title: Nolan's Pocket Notebook
evidence_notebook.desc: Nolan's pocket notebook contains initials, variance notes, and a line about the 'same mark / same kind of scene.'
evidence_notebook.short: Nolan believed this pattern stretched across venues.

evidence_glove_smudge.title: Smudged Glove Print
evidence_glove_smudge.desc: A smeared glove print suggests the victim or equipment was handled after impairment.
evidence_glove_smudge.short: Somebody touched the scene after control was already lost.

evidence_stairwell_scuff.title: Stairwell Scuff Pattern
evidence_stairwell_scuff.desc: Uneven scuffing on the staff stairwell suggests Nolan was descending without normal balance.
evidence_stairwell_scuff.short: Nolan was not moving normally on his way down.

evidence_label_strip.title: Crushed Inventory Label Strip
evidence_label_strip.desc: A crushed beverage inventory label strip is found along Vanessa's likely service route.
evidence_label_strip.short: Vanessa's stock-handling route overlaps the murder route.

evidence_missing_keycard.title: Missing Utility Keycard
evidence_missing_keycard.desc: A utility keycard is unaccounted for during the event.
evidence_missing_keycard.short: A missing access card creates suspicion without proving murder.

evidence_signout_sheet.title: Falsified Sign-Out Sheet
evidence_signout_sheet.desc: Marcus falsified a maintenance sign-out sheet earlier in the week.
evidence_signout_sheet.short: Marcus is dishonest, but dishonesty is not homicide.

evidence_deleted_texts.title: Deleted VIP Texts
evidence_deleted_texts.desc: Elise deleted texts tied to guest perks and off-book comps.
evidence_deleted_texts.short: Embarrassing and risky, but not necessarily murderous.

evidence_private_call_gap.title: Private Call Gap
evidence_private_call_gap.desc: Jordan stepped away from post during the disruption for a personal call.
evidence_private_call_gap.short: Jordan lied, but maybe about the wrong thing.

public: Private event is active across Levels 2 and 3.
A brief power flicker causes floor confusion.
Nolan is last seen active on Level 2.
Nolan heads downstairs after receiving a message.
Nolan is found near the Level 1 service corridor by the caddy shack.

notes: Public guest movement is slower and more visible.
Staff routes allow faster travel between floors.
The Level 1 service corridor has meaningful camera blind spots.

scene_201_opening.title: Topgolf After the Flicker
scene_201_opening.text: The place still hums like a machine pretending nothing happened. Music bleeds through three stories of glass, metal, turf light, and late-night money. Nolan Pierce is dead on Level 1 near the caddy shack, and the building is already trying to turn him into an accident.

scene_202_body_reveal.title: Level 1 Service Corridor
scene_202_body_reveal.text: Nolan lies near the caddy shack threshold beside a dented metal cart edge. The corridor is practical, ugly, and built to make accidents believable if nobody studies them too long.
choices: Review the area and suspect pool

scene_203_cart_edge.title: Dented Cart Edge
scene_203_cart_edge.text: The strike point suggests something more controlled than a clean rushing fall. It does not prove murder, but it weakens the romance of an accident.

scene_204_espresso_can.title: Discarded Drink
scene_204_espresso_can.text: A half-finished canned espresso cocktail sits in the nearby service trash with residue in the lip seam. In a place this busy, the most dangerous object is usually the one still pretending to be ordinary.

scene_205_suspect_board_intro.title: Who Had the Building?
scene_205_suspect_board_intro.text: Topgolf gives staff many versions of the same night: guest-facing movement, service routes, clean routes, dirty routes, and quiet routes. Whoever killed Nolan understood that.

scene_207_round1_checkpoint.title: More Than a Fall
scene_207_round1_checkpoint.prompt: What first suggests Nolan's death may not be a simple accident?
answers: The building is crowded and confusing
The drink can and impact geometry do not fit a normal fall
The caddy shack is a dangerous area
Marcus works near the scene
scene_207_round1_checkpoint.correct: The corridor can support an accident story, but the metal strike pattern and the suspicious drink can both push against it.
scene_207_round1_checkpoint.incorrect: That may contribute to confusion, but it does not explain why the physical evidence resists a clean accidental fall story.

scene_208_ava_intro.title: Ava Morales
scene_208_ava_intro.text: Ava still smells like citrus peel and coffee liqueur. She keeps wiping her hands even though they are already clean.

scene_209_darius_intro.title: Darius King
scene_209_darius_intro.text: Darius looks like a man afraid his memory will get somebody arrested if he arranges it wrong.

scene_210_vanessa_intro.title: Vanessa Cole
scene_210_vanessa_intro.text: Vanessa speaks like somebody used to making large nights function while other people congratulate themselves for surviving them.

scene_211_marcus_intro.title: Marcus Reed
scene_211_marcus_intro.text: Marcus has the wrong look for innocence: grease on one sleeve, frustration on his face, and the body practically in his work neighborhood.

scene_212_elise_intro.title: Elise Benton
scene_212_elise_intro.text: Elise stands like the room still belongs to paying guests and she intends to remain part of the décor, not the evidence.

scene_213_trent_intro.title: Trent Holloway
scene_213_trent_intro.text: Trent carries the heat of the kitchen with him like a second skin.

scene_213b_jordan_intro.title: Jordan Pike
scene_213b_jordan_intro.text: Jordan answers questions the way security men answer doors: just wide enough to stay technically cooperative.

scene_214_route_records.title: Movement Records
scene_214_route_records.text: Elevator delay logs, maintenance pings, guest wristband scans, and kitchen order marks all leave behind small mechanical truths. None is perfect alone. Together, they narrow who could move freely and who only sounds mobile because the building is big enough to hide bad storytelling.

scene_214_round2_checkpoint.title: What Matters Here
scene_214_round2_checkpoint.prompt: What matters more in this case than simple motive?
answers: Who shouted at Nolan most recently
Controlled access and believable movement between floors
Who worked nearest the caddy shack
Who had the strongest physical build
scene_214_round2_checkpoint.correct: A multi-floor venue turns movement into evidence. The killer here needed a route, not just anger.
scene_214_round2_checkpoint.incorrect: That may influence suspicion, but this case lives or dies on who could move through the building without breaking their story.

scene_215_priya_intro.title: Priya Nand
scene_215_priya_intro.text: Priya speaks carefully, like she is trying not to accidentally promote a blurred memory into certainty.

scene_216_blind_spot_map.title: Blind Spot Map
scene_216_blind_spot_map.text: The Level 1 back corridor near the caddy shack sits in one of those practical blind seams built into service-heavy venues: enough visibility for efficiency, not enough for certainty.

scene_217_route_synthesis.title: The Quiet Route
scene_217_route_synthesis.text: Marcus has the wrong zone but not the right freedom. Elise moves elegantly, but public guest movement slows her story. Trent has corridor access, but service timestamps pin him down too often. Jordan knows the blind spots, but his lie points toward embarrassment, not orchestration. Vanessa lives in the overlap.

scene_219_round3_checkpoint.title: The Best Covert Path
scene_219_round3_checkpoint.prompt: Which suspect had the most believable covert path from drink handoff to the Level 1 staging area?
answers: Vanessa Cole
Marcus Reed
Elise Benton
Jordan Pike
scene_219_round3_checkpoint.correct: Vanessa combines drink access, plausible cross-floor movement, and the kind of practical cover that makes staff movement disappear.
scene_219_round3_checkpoint.incorrect: That suspect may have access, but the cleanest full route belongs to the person who could touch both the drink and the quiet Level 1 corridor without seeming out of place.

scene_220_raymond_intro.title: Raymond Sloane
scene_220_raymond_intro.text: Raymond has spent enough years around facility accidents to resent how quickly people use the word accident when they want work to stay simple.

scene_221_forsythe_intro.title: Dr. Lena Forsythe
scene_221_forsythe_intro.text: Dr. Forsythe does not sound impressed by dramatic theories. That makes her immediately useful.

scene_222_stairwell_trace.title: Staff Stairwell Trace
scene_222_stairwell_trace.text: Scuffs on the staff stairwell read uneven and off-balance, and a smudged glove trace suggests contact that was functional rather than random. Not a fight. Not a sprint. A guided failure.

scene_223_round4_checkpoint.title: Before the Metal Edge
scene_223_round4_checkpoint.prompt: What happened before Nolan struck the metal edge?
answers: He slipped while running alone
He was already chemically impaired and being guided
He fought Marcus in the corridor
He blacked out from stress and exhaustion
scene_223_round4_checkpoint.correct: The body, stairwell trace, corridor sighting, and medical view all point the same way: control was lost before impact.
scene_223_round4_checkpoint.incorrect: That theory fails to explain the guided movement, the compromised descent, and the medical sequence before the strike.

scene_224_records_reveal.title: What Nolan Was Building
scene_224_records_reveal.text: Inventory variance sheets. Initials. Event-night beverage gaps. Ledger images. Notes about staff traffic, noise cover, and accident framing. And on one photographed page: the same black symbol already tied to another nightlife death. This was bigger than theft.

scene_225_vanessa_followup.title: Vanessa Revisited
scene_225_vanessa_followup.text: Vanessa remains composed longer than most guilty people do. That is because she has had practice being useful while danger stood nearby.

scene_226_case_synthesis.title: Useful Enough to Kill
scene_226_case_synthesis.text: Nolan was lured downstairs. He likely drank the compromised can before descending. He did not travel normally. Someone guided him through a blind service route. The final impact was staged to look like building chaos. The victim had proof of event-night theft and the same symbol pattern. Among all suspects, only Vanessa sits comfortably inside every layer.

scene_227_round5_checkpoint.title: Why Nolan Died Tonight
scene_227_round5_checkpoint.prompt: Why was Nolan killed that night?
answers: He embarrassed Marcus in front of staff
He had enough proof to expose Vanessa's theft and her connection to the larger pattern
He was about to report Elise's guest perks
He threatened Jordan's job
scene_227_round5_checkpoint.correct: The murder had urgency because Nolan's proof had finally become dangerous to more than one secret at once.
scene_227_round5_checkpoint.incorrect: That explains tension, but not the layered urgency. The real motive must account for theft, exposure, and the larger symbol pattern Nolan was tracing.

scene_228_final_accusation.title: Name the Killer
scene_228_final_accusation.text: The building keeps running. Screens still glow. Bays still buzz. Staff still move. A man is dead in the machinery beneath the performance and Topgolf is already trying to become a venue again. Now you decide whether the building gets its accident — or the truth.

ending_perfect.title: Perfect Solve
ending_perfect.text: You did not let the building keep its lie. Vanessa Cole used competence as camouflage. She touched the drink, shaped the route, and let service logic carry Nolan Pierce straight into a corridor built to look boring. But boredom is one of the oldest disguises violence has. The arrest holds, and Nolan's notes reveal the same black symbol from the first case. The first case was a warning. This one is a system.

ending_solved.title: Case Solved
ending_solved.text: You got there. Vanessa Cole drugged Nolan's drink, redirected him through the building, and staged the final impact in a service corridor designed to make ugly things look procedural. Nolan's records reveal this was part of a recurring nightlife pattern.

ending_partial.title: Close, But Incomplete
ending_partial.text: You found structure where the venue wanted confusion. The drugged drink, the route, the impaired descent, and the bad accident geometry were all there — but not tightly enough. Nolan's notes suggest this venue was only one stop on a larger circuit.

ending_failed.title: Wrong Suspect
ending_failed.text: You let the building keep the easier version. Nolan was not killed by the loudest grudge or the nearest work zone. He was killed by someone useful enough to vanish inside operations. The same black symbol appears again in his notes.

interview_ava.name: Ava Morales
interview_ava.intro: I've replayed that bar handoff five times in my head already. Every version annoys me.

topics.label: Nolan's Drink
topics.response: He took a canned espresso cocktail. Not weird for him on a late event night.

topics.label: Vanessa Nearby
topics.response: Yes, Vanessa was near him. Briefly. It looked normal at the time — one of those quick staff exchanges guests never notice.

topics.label: Can Details
topics.response: The can matched what we had up there. That doesn't make it innocent. It makes it convenient.

topics.label: Power Flicker
topics.response: You don't need a blackout for a bad plan to work. You just need ten seconds of people checking the wrong thing.

interview_darius.name: Darius King
interview_darius.intro: I saw him moving fast. That's the part I know. The rest is me trying not to lie by accident.

topics.label: Heading Downstairs
topics.response: Nolan looked at his phone and changed direction almost immediately. He moved like somebody responding to a problem, not wandering into one.

topics.label: Where Did You Think He Was Going?
topics.response: My first thought was Marcus or some Level 1 equipment issue. But that's my assumption, not a fact.

topics.label: Nolan's Demeanor
topics.response: Focused. Annoyed maybe. Not panicked. Definitely not stumbling yet.

interview_priya.name: Priya Nand
interview_priya.intro: I thought I saw a staff member helping somebody. That's what makes this so ugly.

topics.label: What You Saw
topics.response: A staff-clad figure guiding another person through the Level 1 back corridor. The second person looked off-balance.

topics.label: Clothing
topics.response: Dark staff black. Practical. The kind of clothing that disappears in service spaces.

topics.label: Could You See the Face?
topics.response: Not clearly. If I pretend otherwise, I'll only make the case worse.

topics.label: Help or Force?
topics.response: At a glance, it looked like helping. In hindsight, that may have been the disguise.

interview_raymond.name: Raymond Sloane
interview_raymond.intro: I know what accidents around equipment usually look like. This one argues with me.

topics.label: Scene Geometry
topics.response: The strike point and body relationship are off. Not impossible for an accident, just less natural than people want it to be.

topics.label: Cart Edge
topics.response: That edge took force, yes. But the path into it feels shaped. Not like a clean uncontrolled rush.

topics.label: Glove Smudge
topics.response: A smudge like that suggests contact after things were already going wrong. Functional contact.

topics.label: Blind Spot Corridor
topics.response: That corridor is the kind of place where staff movement looks boring enough to go unquestioned.

interview_forsythe.name: Dr. Lena Forsythe
interview_forsythe.intro: Let's not overcomplicate this. The body is already doing enough of the talking.

topics.label: Cause Sequence
topics.response: The fatal impact is real, but the evidence suggests Nolan was compromised before it occurred.

topics.label: Incapacitation
topics.response: He likely lost coordination before the strike. Whether through sedation, contamination, or another ingestive mechanism, the body was not functioning normally.

topics.label: Drink Delivery
topics.response: A canned drink is a perfectly practical delivery method if someone wanted dosage without spectacle.

topics.label: Stairwell Pattern
topics.response: Those scuffs support impaired descent. He was not moving like a healthy man in a hurry.

interview_vanessa.name: Vanessa Cole
interview_vanessa.intro: If you need someone to explain how a large event venue operates under pressure, I'm happy to help. If you need someone to absorb a panic theory, I have less time for that.

topics.label: Your Route
topics.response: I was moving between Level 2 beverage coordination and event-side support. Exactly where my job usually places me when a building loses rhythm.

topics.label: Nolan Pierce
topics.response: Competent, controlling, and occasionally too interested in proving he saw patterns other people missed.

topics.label: Inventory Issues
topics.response: Inventory issues happen in venues like this. Product moves fast. Staff make mistakes. Anybody telling you normal variance equals corruption is overselling the mystery.

topics.label: Drink Handoff
topics.response: If Ava saw me near Nolan while he grabbed a canned drink, then yes, that is an ordinary workplace moment.

topics.label: Press Further: Elevator Delay
topics.response: Logs don't capture real-time staff improvisation. People take stairs. People reroute. That's what operations work is.

topics.label: Press Further: The Symbol
topics.response: A symbol on a ledger page is not a criminal confession. Nolan was building a mythology out of fragments.

interview_marcus.name: Marcus Reed
interview_marcus.intro: You're looking at me because of the body location. That's lazy, but I get it.

topics.label: Your Work Zone
topics.response: Level 1 support, caddy-side, utility runs when needed. Which means yes, this is my zone. Still not my body.

topics.label: Nolan Pierce
topics.response: He liked paperwork more than people. He wrote me up before. Didn't make me homicidal.

topics.label: Maintenance Call
topics.response: I got a real work ping. Check whatever system you're already checking. I was dealing with actual gear.

topics.label: Press Further: Falsified Sign-Out Sheet
topics.response: I fudged paperwork before. That's not admirable, but it's not murder either.

topics.label: Press Further: Timing
topics.response: I looked bad because of where he landed, not because I was free enough to build the whole thing.

interview_elise.name: Elise Benton
interview_elise.intro: If this turns into a morality play about VIP culture, at least have the courtesy to make it stylish.

topics.label: Your Route
topics.response: Level 3 to Level 2, guests to bar, bar back to bays. VIPs move slower than staff and complain louder.

topics.label: Conflict with Nolan
topics.response: Nolan disliked anything he couldn't itemize. That included me.

topics.label: Press Further: Off-Book Comps
topics.response: I protected guests. That is what venues pay pretty people to do after they stop saying it out loud.

topics.label: Wristband Record
topics.response: The building remembers enough of my route to prove I was doing visible work murderers usually avoid.

interview_trent.name: Trent Holloway
interview_trent.intro: Kitchen problems always become everyone's personality by midnight. Now apparently they also become motive.

topics.label: Kitchen Window
topics.response: I was handling a backlog because event timing and kitchen reality are not the same thing.

topics.label: Service Corridor Access
topics.response: Of course I use the corridor. Half the building does if they actually work here.

topics.label: Conflict with Nolan
topics.response: He liked blaming kitchen for anything guests could see. I liked being too busy to care.

topics.label: Order Rail Snapshot
topics.response: The rail doesn't care whether you think I seem angry enough. It cares where I was needed.

interview_jordan.name: Jordan Pike
interview_jordan.intro: Security hears one truth from cameras, another from staff, and a third from management.

topics.label: Your Route
topics.response: Stairwells, elevator bank, guest redirection during the flicker. Standard float coverage.

topics.label: Blind Spots
topics.response: Every building has them. Pretending otherwise is a management habit, not a security one.

topics.label: Conflict with Nolan
topics.response: He thought security should prevent everything. That's not how buildings or people work.

topics.label: Press Further: Private Call Gap
topics.response: I stepped away for a call I should have ignored. That's the truth. It also isn't murder.

floors.label: Level 3
floors.subtitle: VIP traffic and visible guest movement

zones.label: VIP Bays
notes: Elise's claimed guest route starts here.
Movement here is public and slower.

zones.label: Private Event Space
notes: High guest visibility.
Poor place for covert staff handling.

zones.label: Upper Bar
notes: Crowded but visible.

zones.label: Stairs Down
notes: Fast route to Level 2/1 for staff.

floors.label: Level 2
floors.subtitle: Drink handoff floor and pivot point

zones.label: Main Bays
notes: Open guest-facing area.

zones.label: Level 2 Bar
notes: Ava saw Nolan take the canned espresso cocktail here.
Vanessa was near Nolan during the drink window.

zones.label: Event Walkway
notes: Darius saw Nolan react to a message and change direction.

zones.label: Staff Stairs
notes: Most believable covert route downward after the handoff.

zones.label: Elevators
notes: Delay logs weaken Vanessa's elevator-route claim.

routes.label: Vanessa's likely route
notes: Drink handoff at Level 2 Bar.
Quick pivot through event walkway.
Down staff stairs instead of delayed elevator.

routes.label: Nolan's path after the message
notes: Darius saw Nolan redirect after reading the message.
Victim heads downstairs before visible impairment becomes obvious.

zones.label: Lobby
notes: Too public for a covert attack.

zones.label: Manager Office
notes: Administrative zone.

zones.label: Kitchen
notes: Trent's claimed area; timestamps partially support him.

zones.label: Service Corridor
notes: Priya saw a staff-clad figure guiding a stumbling person here.
Camera blind seam makes quiet movement easy.

zones.label: Caddy Shack
notes: Body found near this threshold.
Marcus looks bad because this is his zone.

zones.label: Utility Closet
notes: Missing keycard red herring hangs near this area.

zones.label: Staff Stairs Exit
notes: Stairwell scuff pattern suggests impaired descent.

routes.label: Guided movement to the scene
notes: Impaired descent emerges from stairwell.
Priya's sighting fits guided movement, not a free stumble.
Route ends at staged impact near caddy shack threshold.
